purpose: Visible page-level summaries for search snippets and AI/RAG extraction
claimPolicy: Precise, externally verifiable credential wording; research supports subject expertise and tutoring credibility; tutoring pages remain primary conversion routes.

capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/index.html: Dr Nicky Grant provides specialist online tuition in A Level Maths, Further Maths, A Level Economics, TMUA, Oxbridge Economics, university economics, econometrics, statistics and finance. His credentials include a Cambridge BA, MPhil and PhD in Economics, university teaching experience and published economics/econometrics research.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/services.ht: The services page helps visitors choose between A-Level/admissions support and university/postgraduate support.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/econometric: Econometrics support is for students who need help with a module, problem set, dissertation, software output or research-methods question.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/a-level-mat: Dr Nicky Grant offers online A Level Maths and Further Maths tuition for UK students. Support covers pure mathematics, statistics, mechanics, proof, calculus, functions, algebra, graphs and TMUA-style reasoning.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/a-level-eco: Dr Nicky Grant offers online A Level Economics tuition for UK students. Support covers microeconomics, macroeconomics, diagrams, quantitative economics, data response, essay structure, evaluation and exam preparation.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/tmua-prepar: Dr Nicky Grant offers online TMUA preparation for students applying to Cambridge, Warwick, Durham and other quantitative courses. Support covers Paper 1, Paper 2, logic, functions, proof, probability, graphs, timing and mathematical reasoning.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/tmua-questi: A free set of 50 TMUA practice questions with full worked solutions, graded by difficulty from D2 to D5 across Paper 1 (Mathematical Thinking) and Paper 2 (Mathematical Reasoning). Each question is multiple choice with instant marking and a complete solution. It is a free sample from a new TMUA practice app of 2,000 questions currently in development by Dr Nicky Grant.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/oxbridge-ec: Dr Nicky Grant offers online Oxbridge Economics preparation for students applying to Cambridge, Oxford and related economics degrees. Support covers interview reasoning, mathematical economics, problem solving, personal statements and application strategy.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/university-: Dr Nicky Grant offers online university economics tuition for undergraduate and postgraduate students. Support covers microeconomics, macroeconomics, mathematical economics, econometrics, statistics, dissertations, problem sets and exams.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/statistics-: Dr Nicky Grant offers online statistics tuition for economics, finance and social science students. Support covers probability, distributions, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, regression, inference, data interpretation and econometrics foundations.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/finance-tut: Dr Nicky Grant offers online finance and quantitative finance tuition for students who need stronger economics, statistics and econometrics foundations. Support covers asset pricing, financial econometrics, time series, volatility, risk, returns and empirical finance.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/financial-e: Dr Nicky Grant offers online financial econometrics tuition for students studying asset returns, ARMA models, maximum likelihood, ARCH/GARCH volatility, forecasting and empirical finance. Free linked materials cover time series and volatility modelling.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/phd-econome: Dr Nicky Grant offers PhD econometrics support for research students working with econometric theory, identification, GMM, instrumental variables, time series, panel data, asymptotics, simulation, empirical strategy or thesis methods chapters.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/economics-s: Dr Nicky Grant provides online support across economics, statistics and econometrics for students who need help connecting theory, mathematical notation, empirical methods and data interpretation.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/credentials: Credentials are summarised for parents and students who want to check qualifications, public profiles and teaching background before enquiring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/research.ht: The research page lists selected publications and public records for students checking advanced econometrics and research-methods background.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/teaching.ht: The teaching page provides free notes, exercises and videos for university students working through econometrics and statistics topics.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/about.html: The about page explains Dr Nicky Grant's teaching approach, academic background and which route to choose.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog.html: Resources are organised by A-Level/admissions and university routes so students can find the most relevant guide quickly.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/pricing.htm: This pricing page explains how to arrange online tuition with Dr Nicky Grant, including subject routes, consultation, payment and the difference between school-level, university-level and advanced econometrics support.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/questions.h: This FAQ page answers common questions about online tutoring with Dr Nicky Grant, including subjects, levels, applications, university economics, econometrics, statistics, finance, TMUA and how to book a consultation.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/econometric: This guide explains the scope of econometrics and links core topics such as OLS, inference, time series, GMM, panel data and financial econometrics to the relevant tutoring routes and free resources.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/choosing-an: This guide explains how students and parents can compare economics, maths and econometrics tutors using subject fit, verified credentials, university-level experience, availability, pricing and direct contact.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/why-choose-: This page explains why direct tuition can be preferable to agency matching for students who need specialist economics, econometrics, maths, statistics, finance, TMUA or Oxbridge Economics support.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/community.h: This page links Dr Nicky Grant’s student-facing tutoring site with broader academic, teaching and public-resource evidence, while keeping the main tutoring routes clear for students and parents.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/what-i: OLS is the core regression method used to estimate a linear relationship between variables by choosing the line that minimises the sum of squared residuals. This guide connects OLS to econometrics, statistics and university-level tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/hypoth: Hypothesis testing compares sample evidence with a null hypothesis using a test statistic, critical value or p-value. This guide supports statistics and econometrics students who need clearer inference methods.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/time-s: Time series econometrics studies data ordered over time, including stationarity, white noise, AR and MA processes, autocorrelation and forecasting. This guide links to econometrics and financial econometrics tutoring.

ARMA models combine autoregressive and moving-average dynamics to describe stationary time series. This guide links stationarity, lag operators and model selection to econometrics and PhD econometrics support.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/ar-arm: AR, MA and ARMA processes are basic models for time-dependent data. This guide explains their structure, stationarity and autocorrelation, with links to time series and financial econometrics tuition.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/readin: A correlogram plots sample autocorrelations across lags and helps diagnose time dependence, model order and residual serial correlation. This guide links to econometrics and financial econometrics tuition.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/garch-: GARCH models describe time-varying volatility, especially in financial returns. This guide links volatility clustering, persistence, maximum likelihood and forecasting to financial econometrics and finance tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/hardes: The hardest Further Maths topics often involve proof, complex numbers, matrices, differential equations, calculus and abstraction. This guide links these areas to A Level Maths and TMUA preparation.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/a-leve: A complete guide to OCR A-Level Mathematics (H240): the structure of Papers 1-3, the AO1/AO2/AO3 assessment objectives and weightings, where students lose marks in examiner reports, and original worked exam-style questions. Links to A-Level Maths and TMUA tutoring.

A complete guide to Edexcel A-Level Mathematics (9MA0): the structure of Papers 1, 2 and 3, the AO1/AO2/AO3 assessment objectives and weightings, where students lose marks in examiner reports, and original worked exam-style questions. Links to A-Level Maths and TMUA tutoring.

A complete guide to AQA A-Level Mathematics (7357): the paper structure, assessment objectives and weightings, the topics where students lose marks, and original worked exam-style questions. Links to A-Level Maths and TMUA tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/furthe: A complete guide to OCR A-Level Further Mathematics (H245): the paper structure, compulsory and optional content, the hardest topics, assessment objectives, and original worked exam-style questions. Links to Further Maths and TMUA tutoring.

A complete guide to Edexcel A-Level Further Mathematics (9FM0): Core Pure and the optional papers, assessment objectives and weightings, common mark losses, and original worked exam-style questions. Links to Further Maths and TMUA tutoring.

A complete guide to AQA A-Level Further Mathematics (7367): the three-paper structure, compulsory and optional content, assessment objectives, the topics that lose marks, and original worked exam-style questions. Links to Further Maths and TMUA tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/a-leve: A complete guide to OCR A-Level Economics (H460): the three papers, question types, AO1-AO4 assessment objectives and weightings, where students lose marks, and essay and data-response technique with original exam-style practice. Links to A-Level Economics tutoring.

A complete guide to Edexcel A-Level Economics A (9EC0): the structure of Papers 1-3, AO1-AO4 assessment objectives and weightings, where students lose marks, and essay technique with original worked exam-style questions. Links to A-Level Economics tutoring.

A complete guide to AQA A-Level Economics (7136): the three papers including 25-mark essays and Paper 3 multiple choice and case study, AO1-AO4 assessment objectives and weightings, where students lose marks, and original exam-style practice with mark guidance. Links to A-Level Economics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/cambri: Cambridge Economics interviews test mathematical reasoning, economic intuition and how students think through unfamiliar problems. This guide links interview preparation to Oxbridge Economics and TMUA tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/oxbrid: This guide works through Oxbridge Economics interview-style questions in microeconomics, game theory, maths reasoning and data interpretation, with links to interview and TMUA preparation.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/econom: A strong economics personal statement should show analytical interest, relevant reading, mathematical readiness and clear motivation for the subject. This guide links application writing to Oxbridge Economics support.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/gauss-: The Gauss-Markov theorem states that, under the classical linear model assumptions, OLS is the Best Linear Unbiased Estimator (BLUE): the linear unbiased estimator with the smallest variance. This guide links to econometrics and statistics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/goodne: R-squared measures the share of variation in the dependent variable explained by a regression, while adjusted R-squared penalises extra regressors. This guide explains their meaning and limits for econometrics and statistics students.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/diagno: Diagnostic tests check the OLS assumptions, including heteroskedasticity (White, Breusch-Pagan), serial correlation (Durbin-Watson, Breusch-Godfrey) and functional form (RESET). This guide links regression diagnostics to econometrics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/autoco: Autocorrelation (serial correlation) is correlation between regression errors over time; it leaves OLS unbiased but invalidates ordinary standard errors, so use HAC (Newey-West) errors. This guide links to econometrics and time series tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/instru: Instrumental variables (IV/2SLS) address endogeneity using instruments that are relevant and excluded; weak instruments bias 2SLS and invalidate inference. This guide links IV and the first-stage F to econometrics and PhD econometrics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/genera: The Generalized Method of Moments (GMM) estimates parameters from population moment conditions, with efficient two-step weighting and the Hansen J test for over-identification. OLS, IV and 2SLS are special cases. This guide links to econometrics and PhD econometrics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/identi: Identification-robust inference (Anderson-Rubin type) stays valid under weak identification by inverting a test of the moment conditions. This guide connects to Dr Grant's own research on the generalised Anderson-Rubin statistic with a singular moment variance matrix.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/confid: A confidence interval gives a plausible range for a parameter and is equivalent to a two-sided t-test at the matching significance level. This guide explains the construction and interpretation of intervals and t-tests for statistics and econometrics students.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/distri: The normal, t, chi-squared and F distributions underpin econometric inference: the normal from the CLT, chi-squared for variances and Wald/LM tests, t for coefficient tests, and F for joint hypotheses. This guide explains how each is used and how they relate.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/maximu: Maximum likelihood estimation chooses the parameter values that make the observed data most probable and is consistent, asymptotically normal and efficient under regularity conditions. This guide links MLE to econometrics, financial econometrics and PhD econometrics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/arma-e: ARMA models are estimated by OLS (AR terms) or maximum likelihood (MA and mixed terms), with order chosen using the ACF/PACF and information criteria (AIC, BIC). This guide links ARMA estimation to time series and financial econometrics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/vector: A vector autoregression (VAR) models several time series jointly on their own and each other's lags, supporting impulse response analysis, variance decomposition and Granger causality. This guide links VAR basics to econometrics and financial econometrics tutoring.
capsules.https://www.drnickygrant.com/blog/modell: Financial returns are mean-zero over short horizons with little autocorrelation but strong volatility clustering, fat tails and excess kurtosis, so heavy-tailed and GARCH models are preferred over the normal. This guide links return distributions to financial econometrics and finance tutoring.

videoPolicy: Teaching videos are listed on teaching.html#teaching-videos, not research.html.

videos.https://www.youtube.com/@economaths: The @economaths YouTube channel hosts 30+ free worked video lectures by Dr Nicky Grant covering applied econometrics: regression theory, matrix methods, asymptotics, inference, instrumental variables, heteroskedasticity, autocorrelation, ARIMA and worked examples. Embedded and indexed on teaching.html.
videos.https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL: Time Series Econometrics: a free 5-video playlist by Dr Nicky Grant on stationarity, white noise, unit roots, ARIMA, autocorrelation and the correlogram, Newey-West (HAC) standard errors and cointegration. Pairs with the site's time-series study notes.

teachingMaterials.https://www.drnickygrant.com/teaching.ht: Free downloadable teaching materials from Dr Grant's university courses in time series and financial econometrics: lecture slides, notes, exercises, problem sets and worked solutions, available as PDFs (/teaching/) and TeX source (/teaching/tex/). Mostly historic materials evidencing teaching style and depth, not current course content.
teachingMaterials.https://www.drnickygrant.com/research.ht: Selected research record: the Generalised Anderson-Rubin singular-variance work with Professor Richard J. Smith (CeMMAP CWP05/19 and the 2025 working paper), the GMM singular-variance paper (2018), the Hausman-volume chapter on many weak instruments (Emerald, 2012) and the tax-price-of-giving article (Springer, 2019). Source TeX files are at /research/tex/.
